postName: Senior Data Scientist – Acme Data Corporation 2025
shortDescription: Apply cutting-edge models and causal inference to turn complex behavioral data into insights that drive hiring decisions.
longDescription:
As a Senior Data Scientist at Acme Data Corporation, you will own the modeling layer of our recruiter-candidate matching platform. Your work will directly determine which candidates surface for which roles — and why.

You will design and evaluate models that go beyond correlation, using causal inference techniques to identify the factors that genuinely predict successful hires. You will collaborate closely with ML Engineering to take your models from research to production, and with the product team to translate insights into features users trust.

This is a high-autonomy role. You will define research directions, set evaluation standards, and be the internal authority on what the data actually says.
jobBenefits:
- Competitive salary and equity
- Fully remote with flexible hours
- Health, dental, and vision insurance
- $2,500 annual learning and conference budget
- Home office stipend

estimatedSalary.comment: Base salary; total comp includes equity

values: Own the end-to-end modeling lifecycle for recruiter-candidate matching, from problem framing through production monitoring
Design observational studies and natural experiments to identify causal drivers of successful placements
Develop and maintain a suite of offline and online evaluation metrics that the team trusts
Partner with ML Engineering to productionize models and ensure they degrade gracefully under distribution shift
Synthesize findings into clear written reports and present recommendations to product and leadership
Mentor junior data scientists and establish best practices for statistical rigor across the team
5+ years of applied data science experience in a production environment
Deep fluency in causal inference methods — propensity scoring, instrumental variables, difference-in-differences, or equivalent
Strong Python skills and hands-on experience with pandas, numpy, scikit-learn, and statsmodels
Track record of shipping models that measurably improved a product metric
Ability to communicate statistical concepts and uncertainty clearly to non-technical audiences
Comfort with ambiguity and experience defining research agendas from loosely specified goals
Experience with large language models or embedding-based similarity for text understanding
Familiarity with recommendation systems or learning-to-rank
Prior exposure to labor market dynamics, HR tech, or two-sided marketplace problems
Experience with A/B testing platforms and experimental design at scale
Publications or open-source contributions in ML, statistics, or econometrics
